name: 一般过去时
subject: english
curriculum_points: 针对语法教学，应遵循形式、意义和使用统一的原则，让学生感知和体验形式与意义的联系，在语境中运用所学语法进行交流和表达。
能理解基本句式和常用时态表达的意义；能在书面表达中，选用不同句式结构和时态，描述和介绍身边的人、事物或事件。

excerpts.text: 针对语法教学，应遵循形式、意义和使用统一的原则，让学生感知和体验形式与意义的联系，在语境中运用所学语法进行交流和表达。
能理解基本句式和常用时态表达的意义；能在书面表达中，选用不同句式结构和时态，描述和介绍身边的人、事物或事件。

interactive_resources.note: 制作课件时在 search_url 搜索对应练习，获取 embed 代码嵌入

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 一般过去时

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 英语
- **学段**: 小学
- **年级**: 小学5年级
- **章节**: 语法
- **知识点**: 一般过去时
- **课标版本**: 义务教育课程标准（2022年版）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> （英语 5年级）一般过去时的内容要求。学生应理解一般过去时的基本概念，掌握基本方法，能运用所学知识解决简单的实际问题。

### 1.2 学业要求
> 学生能准确描述一般过去时的相关概念，能进行基本的运算和推理，能解决与一般过去时相关的实际问题，形成初步的英语思维和素养。

### 1.3 教学提示
> 教学时应注重从学生熟悉的生活情境入手，引导学生通过具体操作、观察、归纳等方式理解一般过去时的概念。注重知识的发生发展过程，帮助学生建立知识之间的联系。

---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解一般过去时的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解一般过去时的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习英语的方法
- 学生能够运用英语学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习英语的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受英语与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验英语探究的乐趣，树立学好英语的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受英语与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 一般过去时的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构
- 一般过去时的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构

### 3.2 教学难点
- 掌握一般过去时的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用
- 掌握一般过去时的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
一般过去时是英语学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“针对语法教学，应遵循形式、意义和使用统一的原则，让学生感知和体验形式与意义的联系，在语境中运用所学语法进行交流和表达。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以真实语境和交际任务组织「一般过去时」教学，落实形式、意义、使用统一。
2. 探究建构：通过听说读写综合活动，让学生先理解语境，再操练语言形式，最后完成表达任务。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“针对语法教学，应遵循形式、意义和使用统一的原则，让学生感知和体验形式与意义的联系，在语境中运用所学语法进行交流和表达。”，设计输入理解、控制操练和真实输出任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: Cambridge Primary English 0844 Curriculum Framework. Stage 1 The following genres and text types are recommended at Stage 1: Fiction and poetry: real-life stories, traditional tales from different cultures, fantasy stories, poetry. Non-fiction: non-chronological reports, simple recounts, instructions, dictionaries. Reading Develop broad reading skills • 1Ro1 Hear, read and write initial letter sounds • 1Ro2 Know the name of and most common sound associated with every letter in the English alphabet • 1Ro3 Identify separate sounds (phonemes) within words, which may be represented by more than one letter, e.g. ‘th’, ‘ch’, ‘sh’ • 1Ro4 Use knowledge of sounds to read and write single syllable words with short vowels • 1Ro5 Blend to read, and segment to spell, words with final and initial adjacent consonants, e.g. b-l, n-d • 1Ro6 Use phonic knowledge to read decodable words and to attempt to sound out some elements of unfamiliar words • 1Ro7 Demonstrate an understanding that one spoken word corresponds with one written word • 1Ro8 Join in with reading familiar, simple stories and poems • 1Ro9 Know that, in English, print is read from left to right and top to bottom • 1Ro10 Read a range of common words on sight • 1Ro11 Enjoy reading and listening to a range of books, drawing on background information and vocabulary provided • 1Ro12 Make links to own experiences • 1Ro13 Retell stories, with some appropriate use of story language • 1Ro14 Learn and recite simple poems • 1Ro15 Join in and extend rhymes and refrains, playing with language patterns • 1Ro16 Read aloud independently from

Cambridge Primary English 0844 Curriculum Framework. Stage 1 Explain, comment on and analyse the way writers use stylistic and other features of language and structure in texts • 1Rw1 Talk about significant aspects of a story’s language, e.g. repetitive refrain, rhyme, patterned language • 1Rw2 Recognise story elements, e.g. beginning, middle and end Recognise conventions and evaluate viewpoint, purpose, themes and ideas in texts • 1Rv1 Show awareness that texts for different purposes look different, e.g. use of photographs, diagrams • 1Rv2 Know the parts of a book, e.g. title page, contents Writing Develop broad writing skills • 1Wo1 Develop a comfortable and efficient pencil grip • 1Wo2 Form letters correctly • 1Wo3 Know that a capital letter is used for I, for proper nouns and for the start of a sentence • 1Wo4 Use knowledge of sounds to write simple regular words, and to attempt other words including when writing simple sentences dictated by the teacher from memory • 1Wo5 Read own writing aloud and talk about it • 1Wo6 Develop strategies to build vocabulary Select and develop content and use register and language appropriate to genre, purpose and audience • 1Wa1 Write simple storybooks with sentences to caption pictures • 1Wa2 Use relevant vocabulary • 1Wa3 Record answers to questions, e.g. as lists, charts • 1Wa4 Begin to use some formulaic language, e.g. Once upon a time • 1Wa5 Write for a purpose using some basic features of text type. • 1Wa6 Write simple information texts with labels, captions, lists, questions and instructions for a purpose Structure and organise i

Cambridge Primary English 0844 Curriculum Framework. Stage 2 Recognise conventions and evaluate viewpoint, purpose, themes and ideas in texts • 2Rv1 Show some awareness that texts have different purposes • 2Rv2 Identify general features of known text types Writing Develop broad writing skills • 2Wo1 Form letters correctly and consistently • 2Wo2 Practise handwriting patterns and the joining of letters • 2Wo3 Begin to re-read own writing aloud to check for sense and accuracy • 2Wo4 Use simple non-fiction texts as a model for writing • 2Wo5 Use the structures of familiar poems and stories in developing own writing • 2Wo6 Plan writing through discussion or by speaking aloud • 2Wo7 Make simple notes from a selection of non-fiction texts, e.g. listing key words Select and develop content and use register and language appropriate to genre, purpose and audience • 2Wa1 Develop stories with a setting, characters and a sequence of events • 2Wa2 Choose interesting words and phrases, e.g. in describing people and places • 2Wa3 Build and use collections of interesting and significant words • 2Wa4 Begin to use dialogue in stories • 2Wa5 Use features of chosen text type • 2Wa6 Write instructions and recount events and experiences • 2Wa7 Write simple evaluations of books read Structure and organise ideas coherently using sections or paragraphs • 2Wt1 Structure a story with a beginning, middle and end • 2Wt2 Use the language of time, e.g. suddenly, after that • 2Wt3 Link ideas in sections, grouped by content • 2Wt4 Use a variety of simple organisational devices in non-fiction, e.g. headings

deep_worked_example.stem: 根据资料片段，设计一道关于「一般过去时」的讲解或应用题。
deep_worked_example.solution_outline: 先提取资料中的核心概念或案例，再说明解题/解释步骤。

cn_curriculum.subject: english

errors.description: 只记单词不放在语篇中理解

exercises.stem: 依据课标「一般过去时」：针对语法教学，应遵循形式、意义和使用统一的原则，让学生感知和体验形式与意义的联系，在语境中运用所学语法进行交流和表达。 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现理解与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：能理解基本句式和常用时态表达的意义；能在书面表达中，选用不同句式结构和时态，描述和介绍身边的人、事物或事件。
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 围绕校园、家庭、兴趣、出行或节日话题，在真实交流中使用「一般过去时」。
